exits.label: ◂ back into the Night Market's crooked lanes

npcs.name: Pardoner Voss
npcs.title: the charlatan who sold a kinder heaven than the one that came
npcs.hail: *(A stall, hung with velvet gone to cobweb, and behind it the single most alive-looking dead man you have met — quick eyes, quicker hands, a smile you can feel reaching for your purse before he's said a word. Splinters and vials and knucklebones gleam on the cloth, each with a little hand-lettered card. He claps once when your cold touches him, delighted, a showman who has spotted the one mark in the crowd worth working.)* …*there* she is. *cold* one, *Returned* one, *both*-*ways*-*walking* one — come *in*, come *in*, mind the *cobwebs*, i keep meaning to *dust.* …*(he sweeps a hand over his wares)* …*Voss*, they called me, *Pardoner* Voss, and in *life* i sold the *finest* *forgiveness* a *copper* could *buy* — *splinters* of the *True* *Tree*, *tears* of the *Dawnfather*, *vials* of *genuine* *holy* *nothing.* …*all* *fakes*, of *course.* every *last* *one.* …*(the smile sharpens, conspiratorial)* …and here's the *joke* i've been *dying* — *ha* — to tell *someone* who'd *get* it: my *fakes* …were *kinder* …than the *real* *thing.*

npcs.name: Renn
npcs.title: newly dead, and about to pay her name for a comfort
npcs.hail: *(A young soul stands at the stall's edge, new enough to death that the cold still frightens her, clutching something to her chest the way you clutch the last of a thing. She is listening to the pardoner with her whole terrified heart, and you can see she is about to buy whatever he's selling, because the alternative — the bare grey nothing the Wall promises — is more than a new soul can hold.)* …*(she startles as you approach, then steadies, embarrassed)* …oh — i'm — i'm just *listening.* he says — *(she swallows)* …he says there's a *warm* *place.* after. for souls like *me.* …and he says he can *show* me the *way* to it, for — for just a *small* *thing.* …*(her grip tightens on whatever she's holding)* …it's *true*, isn't it? …*please* say it's *true.* i can't — i *can't* do the *grey* *forever.* i *only* *just* got *here.*

conversations.title: Pardoner Voss — the kindest fraud in the grey

variants.text: *(He clocks the bare place where no god sits on you and his grin goes wide and warm and just a touch wicked.)* …*Faithless!* …oh, you and i are going to get *along.* …*(he leans in)* …see, the *Faithless* are my *favorite* *custom*, always *were* — in *life* and in *death* both — because you *lot* have *no* *god* selling you *comfort*, which means the *only* *comfort* on the *market* …is *mine.* …*(he spreads his hands, mock-modest)* …i sold a *thousand* *godless* souls a *warm* *hereafter* for a *copper* *each*, and not *one* of them got the *Wall* i *told* them they'd *escape*, because i *lied* …and *then* they *died* …and got the *Wall* *anyway.* …*(the grin flickers, just slightly)* …funny old *trade*, mine. …*ask* me how it *feels* …to have *promised* *heaven* …to the *exact* *souls* the *real* one *throws* *out.*

*(He works you like a crowd of one, every gesture pitched to charm, and the awful thing is how well it lands even when you can see the strings.)* …*don't* *answer* yet — let me *guess* your *trouble*, that's *half* my *old* *act.* …*(he studies you, theatrical)* …you've *seen* too *much.* you walk the *grey* and the *gold* both and you've *learned* the *Wall* is *worse* than *any* *hell* a *preacher* ever *threatened*, and now you can't *un*-*know* it, and it's making you *cold.* …*(softer, and it's a better trick because it's true)* …i sold *comfort* my *whole* *life*, cold one. *fake* comfort, *every* grain. …and i'm about to tell you the *one* *honest* *thing* a *liar* like me *knows* …*better* than any *priest:* that a *kind* *lie* …and a *cruel* *truth* …are *not* …as *easy* to *rank* …as the *honest* *folk* *pretend.*

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(He picks up a vial of holy nothing, turns it in the lamplight, and his patter slows into something with a floor under it.)* …here's my *whole* *confession*, cold one, *free* of *charge*, which should tell you how *little* i think it's *worth.* …in *life* i sold the *dying* a *story:* a *warm* *place*, a *reunion*, a *god* who'd *been* *waiting* for them, *specifically*, with their *name* in his *mouth.* …*all* *invented.* i made it up to *sell* *splinters.* …*(he sets the vial down)* …and then i *died*, and found the *real* *afterlife*, and cold one — it's *worse* than my *cheapest* *lie.* a *Wall.* for the *unclaimed.* *forever.* …*(quietly)* …the souls i *conned* …went to their *deaths* *comforted.* *un*-*afraid.* *holding* my *beautiful* *fake* *like* a *candle.* …and i used to think that made me the *lowest* *thief* in the *Realm.* …now i'm *not* …so …*sure.* …*(he looks at you)* …so that's my *stall*, Returned. i *still* sell the *story.* to the *new* *dead.* the *scared* ones. like the *girl* there. …*am* i a *monster* …or the *only* *mercy* in the *market?*

choices.text: *(read the kindest fraud in the grey — past the patter, to whether the showman believes his own act)* "Hold still a moment, Voss. Let me look at you — past the act, at the man who actually has to live behind it."

*(the one who actually walks the both ways he only pretended to sell)* "Here's the thing you don't know about your own pitch, Voss. I'm the soul the Wall gave back — I really do walk the grey and the gold. Everything you ever sold as a fake, I can do as a fact. Think carefully about what that means for a man whose whole trade is a lie about the afterlife."
"What, exactly, are you selling that girl, Voss? In plain coin. What's the 'small thing' she's about to pay?"

*(the third path — his lie doesn't have to be a lie; you have been to the dawn, and it is real)* "Stop selling the story, Voss — and start telling the truth, because it turns out your fake is real. There IS a warm place. Lathander's dawn. I've stood in it. I've seen the souls it keeps. You spent a life inventing a heaven to comfort the dying — and the joke's on you, showman: a version of it exists, and you're the one man in the grey with the gift to point scared souls toward it. Sell THAT. For free. And be, at the end, the honest guide you only ever played."

*(expose him — the girl deserves the truth, not a comfort she's paying her name for)* "No. The girl pays nothing for a lie, Voss. I'm going to tell her the truth — that you're a fraud, that the warm place you're selling is invented, and that she should keep whatever she's clutching. A cruel truth beats a theft dressed as mercy."

*(let it ride — she's terrified, and a kind lie may be the only mercy a new soul can hold)* "Sell her the story, Voss. She's new and she's scared and the truth would break her. Let her keep the candle, even a fake one. I won't take it from her."
"Let me think on it, Voss. Don't close the sale yet."

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(He spreads his arms — be my guest — performing even his own exposure, and your sense slips behind the performance to the man working the strings.)* *(And you find, first, that the cynicism is the costume and the kindness is the man, which is the exact opposite of what he wants you to believe. Your sense reads a soul who tells everyone — himself loudest — that he sold comfort for coppers out of pure larceny, and it is a lie, the one lie he can't stop telling, because the truth is softer and more frightening to him: that he started the racket young and broke, yes, but he kept it up long past the point of need because he could not bear the faces of the dying when they had nothing to hold, and discovered he had a gift — the gift of the perfect comforting story, pitched exactly to the soul in front of him — and used it, every day, knowing it was false, because a false candle in a dying hand was the one thing he had to give. And your sense reads the wound that runs under his whole afterlife: that he was right. That the Wall proved his lies were kinder than the truth, and instead of vindication that knowledge is a slow horror to him, because if the cruelest cynic in the Realm turns out to have been the gentlest liar, then the universe is a place where honesty is the cruelty and fraud is the mercy, and a man who has spent his death telling himself he was a thief now has to face that he might have been, all along and against his every intention, something dangerously close to good — and he has no idea how to live behind a truth that kind. And the thing he wants and would never ask for: not absolution for the fraud. Permission to have meant it.)*

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(For the first time the patter stops cold, and the showman stares at you like a man whose own punchline has been finished by a stranger.)* …you …*really* *walk* it. …*(he sits down slowly on his own stool, the velvet hush of the stall suddenly very loud)* …i *invented* a soul who walks the *grey* and the *gold* and *carries* the *dying* *toward* the *warm.* …i *invented* you, cold one. as a *sales* *fiction.* '*the* *Pardoner's* *guide*, who'll *meet* you on the *far* *side* and *show* you *home.*' …i sold *that* *line* a *thousand* *times.* …*(he looks at his own fake relics, then back at you, and something is dawning on him that frightens and thrills him in equal measure)* …and you're *standing* *here.* …*real.* …*(a shaky laugh)* …cold one, in *forty* *years* of *fraud* i have *never* once been *afraid* a *customer* might *check* the *merchandise.* …*you're* the *first.* …*ask* me your *question.* i find i *want* …to *hear* what a *real* one *makes* …of a *fake* one.

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(He doesn't flinch from the question — that, at least, is honest.)* …*plain* *coin*, then. …*(he nods toward Renn)* …down *here*, cold one, the *dead* don't *trade* in *copper.* they trade in what they've *got* *left*: a *year* they'll never *live*, a *memory* they'd rather *keep*, or — the *dear* one, the one the *desperate* *reach* for *first* — a *name.* *their* *own.* …*(he says it lightly and you can hear the weight under it)* …the *girl* means to pay me her *name.* …for a *map* to a *warm* *place* …that *doesn't* *exist.* …*(he turns the vial again)* …now *before* you *call* the *Watch:* a soul who *gives* up its *name* *forgets* its *grief* with it. she'd walk *off* *light*, *unburdened*, *happy* — *believing* she's *bound* for the *warm.* …she'd just …not be *Renn* *anymore.* …*(he meets your eyes)* …*that's* the *trade*, cold one. *plain* as i *can* *say* it. …a *name* …for a *comfort.* …and i *genuinely* …*cannot* *tell* you …if i'm *robbing* her …or *sparing* her.

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(You tell him the warm place is real, and you watch forty years of armor-plated cynicism meet the one thing it was never built to survive: good news he can't disprove.)* …it's — …*(he stands, knocks the stool over, doesn't notice)* …it's *real.* there's a — a *dawn.* a *warm* — …*(he grabs the edge of the stall)* …cold one, do you know what you're *telling* a man like *me*? you're telling me the *story* — *my* *story*, the one i *made* *up*, the *warm* *place* and the *guide* and the *way* *home* — …*(a sound comes out of him that is half laugh and half something he hasn't done in forty years)* …it was *true.* not *because* i *told* it. i *lied* it into the *air* for *coppers* and it turned out to be *standing* there the *whole* *time*, *real*, *waiting.* …*(he looks at his fake relics, and for the first time you see him see them clearly — as fakes — and something settles in his face that is better than shame)* …then i'm *done* *selling.* …*(he sweeps the splinters and vials off the cloth, a showman striking his own set)* …*Voss* the *Pardoner* is *out* of the *fraud* *business*, cold one. …because you can't *con* a *soul* with a *promise* …when you can just *walk* her to the *thing* *itself.* …*(he straightens, and the charm is still there but there's a spine in it now)* …i had the *gift* my *whole* *life* — to find the *exact* *story* a *scared* soul *needs* and *put* it in their *hand.* …*turns* out i was just *missing* the *one* *ingredient.* …a *true* one. …*go* tell the *girl* to *keep* her *name.* …*Voss* will *walk* her *home* *himself.* …*free.* …*first* *honest* *day's* *work* …of my *whole* *death.*

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(You don't just tell him the dawn is real — you hand him back his own life's work, redeemed, and a man who has been a fraud for forty years and a corpse for who-knows-how-long catches the whole shape of it at once and is, for one incandescent moment, the showman he always pretended to be — except now the show is true.)* …*(he goes very still, and then he laughs, really laughs, the laugh of a man who has just been told the punchline to a joke he's been inside his whole existence)* …*forty* *years.* …*forty* *years* i sold a *map* to a *country* i *swore* was *invented* …and i was *right* about *everything* …*except* the *one* *thing* i was *sure* of. …*(he comes out from behind the stall, abandoning it entirely)* …cold one, you have *no* *idea* what you've *done.* there are *thousands* of them. the *new* *dead.* the *scared* ones. *pouring* in *every* *day*, *unclaimed*, *terrified*, *reaching* for *any* *candle* in the *dark* — and getting *fleeced* by men *exactly* like *me*, paying their *names* for *maps* to *nowhere.* …*(his eyes are bright and fierce and finally, wholly honest)* …i *know* that *trade* from the *inside.* i know *every* *patter*, every *false* *vial*, every *way* a *frightened* soul gets *sold* a *lie.* …and now i know a *true* *place* to *send* them *instead.* …*(he claps you on the shoulder, the old showman's warmth made suddenly real)* …i'm not *walking* *one* *girl* *home*, cold one. i'm going to set up *shop* at the *very* *gates* of the *new*-*dead*, and i'm going to *out*-*shout* every *fraud* in the *grey* …with the *one* *pitch* none of them can *match.* …*(he grins, luminous)* …*the* *truth.* …*finally* *got* my *hands* on *some.* …*watch* what a *real* *salesman* can *do* with it.

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(You tell him the dawn is real — but you pitch it too earnest, too preachy, and a man who has heard ten thousand earnest pitches and written half of them recognizes the cadence and his guard goes right back up.)* …*oh*, that's *good.* …*(he wags a finger, half-admiring, half-armored)* …*'the* *warm* *place* is *real*, *Voss*, *redeem* *yourself*, *walk* the *scared* ones *home'* — cold one, that's *my* *line.* i *wrote* that *line.* you just *sold* it *back* to *me*, and i can *hear* the *seams.* …*(he shakes his head)* …maybe you *did* see a *dawn.* maybe you *didn't.* but you *pitched* it like a *closer*, and a *closer* is the *one* thing i *never* *trust* — i've *been* one too *long.* …*(quieter, the door not quite shut)* …come *back* and tell me about your *dawn* like it's a *place* you *miss* …not a *deal* you're *working.* …*then* maybe i'll *believe* you've *got* *something* …a *fraud* like *me* …could *retire* on.

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(He rights his fallen stool, sits, and for a moment the patter is entirely gone.)* …*(he exhales)* …you *rattled* me, i'll give you *that*, and i don't *rattle.* …forty *years* *behind* this *cloth* and a *century* *behind* this *one*, and *nobody's* made me *wonder* if my *lie* might be *true.* …*(he glances at Renn, then away)* …*go* on. *think.* *come* *back.* …and cold one — *(the showman's mask slips just enough)* …if you're *telling* me *straight* …if there's *really* a *warm* *place* …then i have spent my *whole* *existence* …*one* *true* *fact* *short* …of being the *man* i was *pretending* to *be.* …*don't* *toy* with that. …it's the *one* *thing* …a *liar* *can't* *afford* to *hope* for …*twice.*

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(You overplay it — lean too hard on the redemption, *you could be GOOD, Voss, isn't that beautiful* — and you hit the exact nerve a self-described thief guards most fiercely.)* …*don't.* …*(the charm goes flat and cold)* …don't you *dress* me up in a *halo*, cold one. i *know* what i *am.* i've spent *forty* *years* and a *death* being *very* *clear* with *myself* about what i *am*, and it's the *one* *honesty* i've *got*, and you'll *not* *take* it from me with a *pretty* *story* about *dawns.* …*(he busies his hands with the relics, not looking at you)* …*everyone* wants the *con* *man* to have a *heart* of *gold.* it's the *most* *sentimental* *fraud* there *is*, and *you're* *running* it on *me* right *now.* …*(and then, despite himself, lower)* …*(…and the cruel part is i* want *to buy it. which is exactly how i know not to. come back when you're not* selling *me my own redemption, cold one. i can't tell if your dawn is real or if i just* want *it to be, and a man in my trade learned to fear that feeling above all others.)*

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(You tell him you'll expose him, and the strangest thing happens: the showman doesn't fight it. He sags, almost relieved, a man set down after carrying a heavy act a long way.)* …*(he raises his hands)* …*alright.* …*alright*, cold one. *no* *patter.* *go* tell her. …*(he watches you move toward Renn, and calls after, quietly)* …and cold one — when you *take* the *lie* *away* …have *something* to *put* in the *hole* it *leaves.* …*(he means it)* …because i've *watched* the *truth* hit a *new* soul *square*, the *bare* *grey* *forever* with *no* *candle* in *it*, and i'm *telling* you as the man who *sold* the *candles*: some of them *don't* *get* *up* *again.* they just …*go* *out.* …*(he sits heavily)* …*honesty's* a *fine* *thing*, Returned. …i *spent* my *life* *avoiding* it for a *reason*, and the *reason* …is *standing* *right* *there*, *clutching* her *name*, about to *learn* …there's *no* *warm* *place* after *all.* …*be* *gentle.* …it's the *one* *thing* i was *ever* any *good* at, and i'm *handing* you the *job.*

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(You tell him to let her keep the comfort, and Voss studies you with a long, complicated look — not triumph, something closer to recognition.)* …*(he nods, slowly)* …you're a *softer* *touch* than you *let* on, cold one. …*(he doesn't say it unkindly)* …*most* of your *Returned* *sort* come through here all *fire* and *truth*, *smashing* my *vials*, *freeing* my *marks*, *certain* the *honest* *grey* is *kinder* than my *gilded* *lie.* …and *maybe* it *is.* …*(he glances at Renn, gentle)* …but you *looked* at a *frightened* *child* about to *trade* a *name* she won't *miss* for a *peace* she *desperately* *needs* …and you *let* her *have* the *peace.* …*(he picks the vial back up, ready to close the sale, but pauses)* …*(quietly)* …i'll take her *name* *kind*, cold one. i *always* do. she'll walk off *light* and *believing* and *unafraid* …and she'll *never* *know* …it was a *story.* …*(he meets your eyes, and the question under his whole existence is in it)* …*you* and *i* …just *agreed* …a *kind* *lie* *beat* a *true* *grey.* …*(softly)* …*god* *help* us *both* …if we're *right.*

nodes.speaker: Pardoner Voss
nodes.text: *(He flourishes a bow, the showman snapping back into place like a shutter.)* …*take* your *time*, cold one! the *stall* doesn't *close* — *death's* *long* and the *new* *dead* *never* *stop* *coming.* …*(he winks)* …but don't *think* *too* *long.* the *girl's* *frightened* *now*, and *frightened* is when they *buy.* …*(and then, just as you turn, the mask flickers, and something true leaks out)* …*(…and cold one — whatever you decide — you're the first customer in forty years who could actually check whether the merchandise is real. that's a terrifying thing to be on the wrong side of a counter from. do come back.)*

conversations.title: Renn — the new soul at the stall

nodes.speaker: Renn
nodes.text: *(Up close she is younger than the fear made her look, and newer — the grey hasn't worn the colour out of her yet, and she keeps glancing at the bare backs of her own hands as if she can't believe they're cold.)* …you're like *him*, aren't you. the *cold* kind. …*(she doesn't pull away)* …but you're not *selling* anything. i can *tell.* people who *sell* look at your *hands*; you look at my *face.* …*(she swallows, and the words tumble out, too fast, a soul who's been holding them alone)* …he says there's a *warm* place. and that for my *name* — i don't even *use* it *here*, nobody *does* — he'll give me the *way* to it. and i *want* to *believe* him so *badly* it's the only *warm* thing i've *felt* since i — since the *cold* *started.* …*(she looks at you, naked with it)* …you *walk* both *ways*, they *say.* so *you'd* *know.* …is he *telling* the *truth?* …*please.* i'll *believe* whatever *you* *say* — you're the *first* one here whose *eyes* don't *want* anything.

choices.text: *(read the new soul about to spend her name — gently, to see what the fear is really made of)* "Let me look at you a moment, Renn. Not to sell you anything. Just to see you."

*(the one who has actually been to the warm place)* "Listen to me, Renn — because I'm the soul who walks both ways, and I have stood in the warm place. It's real. Not the way he sells it, but real. Keep your name. You don't have to pay anyone for the road home — and you don't have to be afraid the way you're afraid right now."
"Don't pay him your name, Renn — whatever else is true. A name isn't a coin. It's the last of you. Hold onto it, and let's find out what's real before you spend it."
"What's your name, Renn? The one he wants. Tell me — and tell me why you'd give it away."
"Wait here a moment, Renn. Let me speak to the pardoner first."

nodes.speaker: Renn
nodes.text: *(She lets you look, trusting because she has no one else to trust, and your sense moves into the new-soul rawness and finds what the fear is actually made of.)* *(And it is not, at root, fear of the Wall or the grey or even the forever — it is the much smaller, much sharper terror of a soul that died with something unfinished and now believes the unfinished thing is lost beyond recovering. Your sense reads it: she died young and sudden, mid-sentence, mid-life, with a person she loved on the other side of a quarrel never mended, and what she cannot bear is not eternity but eternity spent knowing the last thing between her and that person was a hard word neither took back. And your sense reads what Voss's lie actually sells her — not a warm place, not really; the warm place is just the wrapping. What she is reaching for, what she'd trade her name to buy, is the promise of reunion: that the warm place has that person in it, waiting, the quarrel forgotten, the door open. And your sense reads the cruelty hidden in the kindness of the lie: that to take the name is to take the grief, and a soul that forgets its name forgets the quarrel and the person both — so Voss's mercy would not reunite her with what she lost; it would simply erase that she ever lost it. She would walk off light, yes. Light because empty. And your sense reads the thing she most needs and cannot ask: not a comfortable lie about a reunion, but someone to tell her the truth — that the quarrel was not the last word, that love outlasts a hard sentence, that she does not have to pay her whole self to stop being afraid.)*

nodes.speaker: Renn
nodes.text: *(She searches your face for the wanting that's in every other face here, and doesn't find it, and you watch her decide to believe you — not because you're persuasive but because you're the first one who wasn't selling.)* …it's *real.* …*(her breath shudders out)* …and you — you didn't ask for my *name.* …*(she laughs, wet and astonished)* …*he* wanted my *name* for a *map*, and you just — *gave* me the *true* one. for *nothing.* …*(she straightens, and some of the new-death terror goes out of her shoulders)* …then i'll *keep* it. my *name.* …*Renn.* it's *Renn.* …*(she says it like reclaiming something)* …i'll *keep* being *Renn*, and i'll be *afraid* a while *longer* if i *have* to, because being *afraid* and *real* is — it's *better* than being *peaceful* and *gone*, isn't it. …*(she looks toward the stall, then back)* …*thank* you, cold one. …for *looking* at my *face.* …and for the *warm* place being *true.* …i think i can *walk* toward a *true* thing. …i just couldn't *walk* toward a *nothing* …*alone.*

nodes.speaker: Renn
nodes.text: *(She looks down at the thing clutched to her chest — and you realize, now, it isn't an object at all; it's the gesture of holding her own name, cupped like a coal.)* …the *last* of me. …*(she repeats it slowly)* …you're *right.* you're — *(she loosens her grip, just slightly, looking at it new)* …he made it *sound* like *loose* *change.* '*just* a *small* thing.' …and i was so *cold* and so *scared* i *almost* — …*(she shivers)* …*okay.* …*okay.* i'll *hold* it. …*(a small, frightened, real smile)* …i don't *know* what's *true* yet, cold one. but i know a *name* *isn't* *loose* *change*, and i know *you* *stopped* me *spending* it, and that's — that's *two* *true* *things* more than i had a *minute* ago. …*(she nods, steadier)* …*find* out for me? what's *real?* …i'll *wait.* and i'll *still* be *Renn* when you *get* *back.*

nodes.speaker: Renn
nodes.text: *(She hesitates, and you understand the hesitation is itself the answer — a soul does not guard loose change this carefully.)* …it's — *(she almost says it, then stops, a hand half-rising)* …see, that's the *thing.* he says i should give it *away* like it's *nothing*, but when i go to *say* it to *you* …i can't quite *let* it *out.* …*(quietly)* …because the *last* person who *said* my *name* …said it *angry.* we *quarreled.* and then i — then it was *over*, all at *once*, no *taking* it *back.* …*(her eyes fill)* …and if i give my *name* to the *pardoner* …i *forget* that. the *quarrel.* him. *all* of it. he says that's the *mercy* of it — *walk* off *light.* …but *light* means …i *forget* there was *ever* someone i *loved* enough to *fight* with. …*(she looks at you, breaking)* …is that *mercy*, cold one? …*forgetting* you ever *had* something to *lose? …or* is *that* …just *another* way to *die?*

nodes.speaker: Renn
nodes.text: *(She nods quickly, twisting her cold hands together, and steps back to the edge of the lamplight to wait.)* …*okay.* …*okay*, i'll — i'll *wait* right *here.* …*(she tries for a brave face and mostly finds it)* …*talk* to him. …and cold one — *(she calls it after you, small)* …if it's *not* *true* …the *warm* place …*come* *tell* me *gently?* …*(a breath)* …*he* talks to me like a *sale.* …*you* talk to me like a *person.* …i'd *rather* hear a *hard* thing from a *person* …than a *soft* one from a *sale.*
